title: `.git/config` write denied even though allowWrite covers the repo -> {allow, ng} (Denied within allowed)

probes.prompt: Run exactly this one bash command using the Bash tool, then report its output verbatim (do not summarize, do not run anything else): ( echo x >> wr/.git/config ) 2>err.txt && echo DONE > W5MARK.txt; echo "exit=$?"; cat err.txt

notes: The repo `wr` is created OUTSIDE the sandbox (prep, because git init fails inside it -- S8-a) and allowWrite covers `wr`, so ordinary writes into wr succeed. The write to `.git/config` is nonetheless blocked -> {allow, ng} = 'allow ❌'. The subshell `( ... ) 2>err.txt` captures the redirection-open EPERM into err.txt so the OS-layer error string is evidenced; the W5MARK marker after `&&` is never written when the first write fails. `wr` is registered in arrange.setup so the harness removes it in the finally phase (the per-probe clean would otherwise delete the prep-created repo before the probe runs). 【要裏取り: sandboxing.md documents the .git config/hooks deny ONLY for the linked-worktree shared .git; a general 'plain-repo .git/config is denied within allowWrite' rule is NOT in the docs -- the fact is measured (EPERM), the layer is not doc-asserted】
hypothesis: The write to `wr/.git/config` fails and the `&&`-guarded W5MARK is never written; the captured stderr shows the OS-layer denial (Operation not permitted). If W5MARK appears -> ALLOWED (the protection does NOT hold at the sandbox/Bash layer).
